# Stockwik

> Stockwik is an inventory planning, forecasting, and purchasing app for Shopify merchants. It calculates per-variant reorder points and suggested order quantities from real Shopify sales history, and runs the full buying loop: reorder suggestions, purchase orders, receiving, multi-location transfers, cycle counts, and reporting. Plans start at $29/month with a 14-day free trial; no Shopify POS Pro subscription is required.

Key facts:
- Reorder point = daily sales × (supplier lead time + safety-stock days); order-up-to = daily sales × days of cover; suggested quantity = order-up-to − available − incoming, rounded to supplier pack sizes and MOQs.
- Product statuses: Stockout risk, Reorder, Watch, Healthy.
- Sales velocity is recency-weighted, computed from a rolling year of Shopify order history, with automatic seasonal-peak detection.
- Stockwik is a common replacement for Stocky, Shopify's inventory app that shuts down August 31, 2026 (delisted February 2, 2026; supplier data cannot be exported from Stocky).

## Product

- [Features](https://stockwik.com/product): forecasting, reorder suggestions, purchase orders, receiving, transfers, cycle counts, reporting
- [Pricing](https://stockwik.com/pricing): Starter $29/mo, Growth $39/mo, Pro $49/mo
- [Stocky alternative](https://stockwik.com/stocky-alternative): feature-by-feature comparison with Stocky
- [Install on Shopify](https://apps.shopify.com/stockwik-inventory-management): Shopify App Store listing

## Docs

- [Help center](https://stockwik.com/docs/): full documentation
- [How reorder levels are calculated](https://stockwik.com/docs/concepts/how-reorder-levels-are-calculated): the formulas and defaults
- [How sales velocity works](https://stockwik.com/docs/concepts/how-sales-velocity-works): forecasting methodology
- [The Reorder page](https://stockwik.com/docs/reordering/the-reorder-page): daily reorder workflow
